body.title: [sprint] Introduce CapabilityMixin + CodingCapability (thin mixin HAS-A a Domain, delegates)
body.description:
**Intention:** I intend that adding a task-capability to an agent costs one Domain + one thin capability-mixin, never a bespoke agent subclass. This ticket builds the reusable mixin seam.

**What to build:** A new package `unseen_university/capabilities/` with:
- `base.py` — `CapabilityMixin`: a thin mixin that HAS-A a Domain object (composition, NOT is-a — the Domain already stands alone from D-domain-object-encapsulation; the mixin must NOT subclass or re-fuse the Domain). It resolves/holds its domain via `resolve_domain(<name>)` and exposes a single delegation method, e.g. `run_capability(self, ticket, *, agent_id) -> <domain result>` that calls `self._domain.run(ticket, agent_id=agent_id)`. The mixin carries NO task logic of its own — it is glue that exposes a Domain's capability on a host class.
- `coding.py` — `CodingCapability(CapabilityMixin)` bound to domain name `"coding"` (wraps `CodingDomain` via `resolve_domain('coding')`).
- `__init__.py` — export both.

**Do NOT build in this ticket:** PlanningMixin (no consumer yet — would be a speculative empty mixin), the `Agent(BaseDevice)` subclass (stream B), any class rename (stream B). Scope is the capability-mixin mechanism only.

**Completion criteria:** `CodingCapability` composes onto a plain host object and, when `run_capability(ticket, agent_id=...)` is called, delegates to `CodingDomain.run(...)` with the same arguments and returns its result unchanged.

**Test plan (this exercises the NEW seam — real red->green):** unit test that constructs a host mixing in `CodingCapability`, monkeypatches/spies `CodingDomain.run` (or the resolved domain's `run`), calls `run_capability(ticket, agent_id='x')`, and asserts (a) the domain's `run` was called once with the same ticket + agent_id, (b) the return value is passed through unchanged. Red before the mixin exists / green after.

**Affected files:** NEW `unseen_university/capabilities/{__init__,base,coding}.py`; reads `unseen_university/devices/inference/domains/{__init__.py resolve_domain, base.py BaseDomain.run, coding.py CodingDomain}`. No changes to existing domain files.

**Logging:** log the capability->domain delegation crossing at INFO (interface crossing rule).

**Scope boundary:** mechanism only; the worker composition that USES this mixin is T-codebuilder-compose-ds.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Observable via:** `log.info("capability: delegating to domain=coding ticket=<id> agent=<id>")` at the CapabilityMixin.run_capability crossing — this line confirms the seam is live in production.
**Cleanup:** none — the delegation test uses monkeypatch/spy only (no DB rows, files, or channel state).
**Note:** `CapabilityMixin` is an OOP composition mixin, NOT an MCP capability-protocol capability (no handler/shim/skill-consumer surface) — the two-sided-build rule does not apply. Name collision is intentional-but-narrow; keep the docstring explicit.
body.intention: I intend that adding a task-capability to an agent costs one Domain + one thin capability-mixin, never a bespoke agent subclass.
